name: Italian Rum Cake #2
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Beat the yolks and 1/4 cup sugar together in a mixing bowl and slowly add 1/4 cup flour.
3. In a small pot, bring milk to the brink of boiling.
4. Slowly pour the milk over the yolk mixture, then return the whole thing to the pot.
5. Place over medium heat and cook, stirring constantly, until the mixture thickens.
6. Remove from the heat and stir in lemon peel. Transfer to a plastic container, cover, and refrigerate to chill.
7. Cream butter and remaining sugar together in a mixer fitted with a paddle at high speed.
8. Add eggs one at a time, waiting for each to be absorbed before adding the next.
9. Add sour cream and mix until incorporated.
10. Add vanilla, nutmeg, baking soda, salt, flour, and mix on medium speed for an additional minute.
11. Pour batter into a 1-quart round or rectangular cake pan, leaving 1/2-inch space at the top.
12. Place in the middle rack of the oven for 50-to-60 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
13. Remove from oven and let cool for 15 minutes before unmolding onto a rack.
14. Slice cake into 6 layers. Place first layer on a platter and sprinkle with some rum.
15. Cover with custard mixture, place second layer, sprinkle with more rum, and continue covering with additional custard.
16. Lightly spread remaining custard over the surface of the cake.
17. Refrigerate for 2 hours before serving.
